data.title: DEI setback: Nasdaq board diversity rules struck down by appeals court
data.description:
Nasdaq's (NASDAQ:NDAQ) boardroom diversity targets for companies listed on the exchange have been struck down by a federal appeals court, which ruled that the Securities and Exchange Commission did not have the authority to approve such listing rules.

The SEC in 2021 approved a proposed rule requiring Nasdaq-listed companies to have, or explain why they do not have, two diverse directors - women, racial minority or LGBTQ+ people - on their boards by 2026. It also green lit a rule requiring companies to disclose board-level diversity statistics annually.

Two conservative groups - conservative think tank National Center for Public Policy Research and Edward Blum-founded Alliance for Fair Board Recruitment - opposed the approval, arguing that the targets were unlawful, and sued the SEC.

The Fifth U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals ruled in their favor on Wednesday in a narrow 9-8 ruling. "We hold that the diversity rules cannot be squared with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934," the nine judges in the majority wrote, adding that the SEC intruded into territory far outside its ordinary domain.

To note, more than two-thirds of the judges on the Fifth Circuit court were appointed by Republican presidents and the court is considered the most conservative-leaning. in the U.S.

Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) told Reuters that it respects the court's decision and does not plan on seeking further review. The SEC said it was "reviewing the decision and will determine next steps as appropriate." To overturn the ruling, the SEC would have to appeal to the Supreme Court.

The appeals court ruling comes at a time when companies have been quietly scaling back diversity, equity and inclusion policies amid backlash from conservatives.

data.title: European indexes in green, Swiss National Bank cuts rate more than expected
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.10%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.12%

France (CAC:IND) +0.29%

The Swiss National Bank cut its key policy rate by 50 bps to 0.5% today, more than market expectations of a 25 bps cut, marking a fourth consecutive reduction and pushing borrowing costs to the lowest since November 2022. The decision comes amid concerns over the strength of the Swiss franc and low inflation rates.

The annual inflation rate in Sweden stood at 1.6% in November.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.03% lower, as investors watch out for ECB decision today. Analysts see a quarter-point rate cut from the current ECB benchmark rate of 3.25%. A half-point cut “would be a security move to preempt any potential risks for the eurozone economy coming from the next U.S. administration’s potential economic policy choices and political woes in France and Germany,” said Carsten Brzeski, chief eurozone economist at ING bank.

Coming up in the session: The European Central Bank set to announce their interest rate decision at 8.15am ET today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield up 2 basis points to 4.29%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.14%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 4 basis points to 4.36%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Skanska AB divests Arkaden residential project in Lund, Sweden for SEK 520M
data.description:
• Skanska (OTCPK:SKBSY) has announced the divestment of its rental residential project Arkaden in Lund, Sweden, to LF Skåne Hyresfastigheter for approximately SEK 520M.
  • The Arkaden project comprises 139 rental apartments and a grocery store, with about 11,000 square meters of leasable space.
  • According to the Swedish National Board of Housing, Building and Planning's calculation model for climate declarations, the project shall be more than 20% more efficient, achieving energy class B.
  • The transaction will be recorded by Skanska Commercial Development in the fourth quarter of 2024, while transfer of ownership will take place in Q2 of 2027.
  • The construction contract, valued at SEK 340M, will be included in Skanska Sweden's Q4 2024 order bookings.

  • Construction is set to begin in spring 2025 with completion planned for Q2, 2027.

data.title: Autoliv, China's JMC form strategic partnership
data.description:
• Autoliv (NYSE:ALV) to start a new strategic cooperation with Jiangling Motors Co, a Chinese automotive manufacturer, it said Thursday.
  • JMC is a company in China's commercial vehicle industry and a new force in the field of passenger vehicles. Autoliv has supplied seatbelts to JMC since 1997.
  • The new strategic cooperation aims to leverage the companies' respective strengths and enhance global presence together. Efficient supply chain and modularization are expected to create new global market opportunities.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Plantify Foods announces liquidation of subsidiary
data.description:
• Plantify Foods (TSXV:PTFY:CA) said that its wholly owned subsidiary, Peas of Bean (POB) has filed a request with the district court of Nof Hagalil-Nazareth, Israel to initiate proceedings for the purpose of liquidation.
  • Due to the continuous drone attacks, missile strikes and shootings in the region, the area has been almost completely evacuated of civilians. 
  • Plantify Foods (PTFY:CA) has no other business or material assets and intends to commence the evaluation of potential business opportunities to replace POB.

data.title: Financial Institutions prices $95M stock offering
data.description:
• Financial Institutions (NASDAQ:FISI) priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of 4M shares at a public offering price of $25.00 per share.
  • The expected proceeds are ~$95M.
  • Underwriters are granted a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 600K shares.
  • Net proceeds to be used for general corporate purposes which may include a balance sheet restructuring through the repositioning of a substantial portion of our available-for-sale debt securities portfolio to support continued growth as well as the repayment of indebtedness.
  • The stock price dropped 2% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Ormat Technologies prices $281.9M secondary offering on behalf of shareholder ORIX
data.description:
• Ormat Technologies (NYSE:ORA) announces pricing of a secondary offering of 3.7M shares of common stock on behalf of ORIX at $76.20 per share, for expected proceeds of $281.9M.
  • Underwriters' overall allotment is 555,000 shares of Ormat’s common stock from ORIX Corporation.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 13, 2024

  • ORA shares were trading -5.1% after-hours on Wednesday.

data.title: ServiceTitan prices IPO at $71 per share
data.description:
• Software platform that powers trades businesses—ServiceTitan (TTAN) priced its initial public offering of 8.8M shares at a public offering price of $71.00 per share.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.32M shares.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on December 12, 2024, under the ticker symbol “TTAN.”
  • The offering is expected to close on December 13, 2024.

data.title: AmpliTech Group jumps 40%, prices $2.2M direct stock offering
data.description:
•  AmpliTech Group (NASDAQ:AMPG) entered into one or more securities purchase agreements with certain institutional investors to purchase ~1.35M shares at $1.60 per share, in a registered direct offering.

  • The gross proceeds are estimated to be ~$2.2M.

  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 13, 2024.

  • The stock price jumped ~40% on Wednesday during after market hours of trading.

data.title: NewAmsterdam Pharma prices $416M stock offering
data.description:
• NewAmsterdam Pharma Company (NASDAQ:NAMS) priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of ~12.12M at a public offering price of $24.50 per share and pre-funded warrants to certain investors.
  • The proceeds are expected to be ~$416.5M.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.55M shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 13, 2024.
